signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and steady, but the board still marks it no-trade and the edge is weak. With the event veto at CAUTION and macro still RED, there is no reason to force a fresh position here.
ETH remains softer than BTC, with a negative 24h profile and positive funding. It is not a dramatic breakdown setup, but among the majors it still leans better as a fade than as a long.
SOL is weak but not decisively broken. Positive funding hurts the long case, but the downside edge is still too modest to rank it ahead of cleaner shorts like FARTCOIN.
TON has mild positive momentum, but funding is still positive and the mechanical board is only neutral. With CAUTION active, it stays in watch-only territory.
CHILLGUY is up sharply, but the spread is extremely poor and funding is positive, so this is not a clean squeeze continuation. Too unstable to chase under a RED macro regime.
GRASS is basically flat on the day with weak directional structure and poor spread quality. The board does not offer enough asymmetry for either side.
VVV has decent momentum and real liquidity, but funding is still positive and the board does not provide a strong directional bias. Interesting, but not clean enough to outrank the top squeeze names.
FARTCOIN remains one of the cleaner bearish setups: sharp negative 24h trend, positive funding, and strong volume. Among the short candidates, this is one of the more executable fades.
MAV has a mild bullish lean, but liquidity is thin and spread quality is poor. Under CAUTION conditions, that is not enough to justify a fresh long.

TNSR still has a real squeeze-continuation profile with very strong 24h momentum and extremely negative funding. I am haircutting confidence because the event veto is CAUTION and the name is still wide, so this should be treated as half-size only.

BLUR remains one of the best longs on the board: strong upside expansion, negative funding, and better liquidity than most small-cap alternatives. CAUTION status and RED macro keep this as a reduced-size continuation buy, not a full-risk chase.
